channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-Based Kenyan Pleads Guilty to Murder of Former Partner
item.description: A Kenyan man residing in the United States, Nobert Matara, has pleaded guilty to first-degree murder in the death of his former partner, 31-year-old Tracy Nyariki. The incident, which has left both th...

item.title: DMK maintains vote base but suffers key defeats and reduced influence in Tamil Nadu Assembly elections
item.description: The Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), which was in power in Tamil Nadu, experienced a significant setback in the 2026 Assembly elections despite retaining a substantial portion of its core vote base. A...

item.title: California Bill Seeks Privacy Protections for Immigrant Aid Workers Amid Free Speech Debate
item.description: California Assembly Bill 2624 proposes an address confidentiality program for immigrant aid workers, aiming to prevent harassment by prohibiting the posting of their personal information online. Advoc...

item.title: NASA Evaluates Artemis II Mission and Prepares for Artemis III Moon Mission
item.description: Following the successful completion of NASA's Artemis II mission, the agency has begun detailed evaluations of the spacecraft, rocket, and launch infrastructure to prepare for future lunar missions. O...

item.title: Police investigate social media post targeting Maureen Lipman with antisemitic imagery
item.description: Police in Scotland are investigating a social media post by the Scottish Palestine Solidarity Campaign that depicted Jewish actress Maureen Lipman with devil horns and a pitchfork. The post was linked...

item.title: ASX futures fall as Middle East tensions push oil higher
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open lower as global markets react to renewed conflict in the Middle East, particularly following an attack by Iran on an energy facility in the United Arab Emirates....

item.title: High Gas Prices Are Prompting Workers to Seek Shorter Commutes and Remote Work
item.description: As gas prices climb above $6 per gallon in some areas, many Americans are reconsidering their work arrangements. Rising fuel costs are prompting employees to explore jobs with shorter commutes or posi...

item.title: Canadian Dollar Softens Amid Rising Oil Prices, Bond Yields, and Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The Canadian dollar weakened against the U.S. dollar on Monday, influenced by heightened geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and global market movements. Investors grew uneasy over the poten...

item.title: Researchers Develop Solar-Powered Method to Convert Plastic Waste into Hydrogen Fuel
item.description: Researchers at Adelaide University are developing an innovative solar-driven method to convert plastic waste into clean fuels such as hydrogen and other industrial chemicals. Using a process called ph...

item.title: Everton Stage Comeback to Beat Manchester City 2-1 in Premier League Clash
item.description: Everton produced a stunning comeback to secure a 2-1 victory over Manchester City in a Premier League encounter on Monday night. The game, kicked off at 8pm BST, saw Manchester City initially take the...

item.title: Erice urges Mar Roxas to consider 2028 presidential bid
item.description: House Representative Erice has publicly expressed his support for former Interior Secretary Mar Roxas to run for president in the 2028 Philippine national elections. During a recent statement, Erice e...

item.title: Sophia Brous and Paul Grabowsky Create Song Cycle Based on Dorothy Porter’s Poetry
item.description: Nearly two decades after the passing of revered Australian poet Dorothy Porter, her work continues to inspire new artistic interpretations. Sophia Brous, in collaboration with Paul Grabowsky, has deve...

item.title: Mmamoloko Kubayi Rejects ANC's Proposal to Contest for Joburg Mayoral Position
item.description: Mmamoloko Kubayi, South Africa's Minister of Justice and Constitutional Development, has firmly rejected the African National Congress's (ANC) suggestion that she run for the mayoralty of Johannesburg...

item.title: Victoria’s council donation rules leave risk of corruption unchanged
item.description: The article examines the ongoing risk of corruption in Victorian local councils, drawing on past scandals like the City of Casey and Wollongong. In Victoria, there are no caps or bans on donations fro...

item.title: Abia State secures $700m World Bank funding to improve water supply, sanitation, and power infrastructure
item.description: Abia State don secure major financial support from the World Bank under the SURWASH programme, wey worth about $700 million, to improve water supply, sanitation, and hygiene services across the state....

item.title: SAWS Issues Weather Warning for Cold, Wet, and Windy Conditions Across South Africa
item.description: The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has issued a warning for severe cold, wet, and windy conditions affecting large parts of the country from Monday to Thursday. The inclement weather is linked t...

item.title: Hearts defeat Rangers 2-1 to move closer to first league title since 1960
item.description: Hearts secured a remarkable 2-1 victory over Rangers at Tynecastle, putting themselves firmly in contention for a historic Scottish Premiership title. Rangers started strongly and took the lead throug...

item.title: IPI Nigeria to launch new Press Freedom Hub to celebrate World Press Freedom Day
item.description: On May 6, 2026, the International Press Institute (IPI) Nigeria will open a new headquarters and Press Freedom Hub in Abuja. This event is set to mark World Press Freedom Day. The new facility, locate...

item.title: Activist Protests Target Jeff Bezos at Met Gala Over Amazon and ICE Ties
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 Met Gala, co-chaired for the first time by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and his wife Lauren Sánchez Bezos, activist group Everyone Hates Elon has organized a series of creative protests...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Causes Multiple Deaths and Severe Illness
item.description: A rare hantavirus outbreak aboard the Hondius, a polar expedition cruise ship travelling from Argentina to Europe, has resulted in the deaths of three passengers and serious illness in several others....
